integrations_researched.name: Google Gemini API (google-genai SDK)

verified_package.name: google-genai
verified_package.notes: The OLD package 'google-generativeai' is DEPRECATED (support ended Nov 30, 2025). Must use 'google-genai' (the unified Google Gen AI SDK).

api_patterns.initialization: client = genai.Client(api_key='YOUR_API_KEY')  # or auto from GEMINI_API_KEY env var

structured_output.description: Native structured output via response_json_schema config parameter
structured_output.notes: Works with Pydantic BaseModel.model_json_schema(). Supported on Gemini 2.5 and 3.x models.

api_patterns.verified_against: Google AI for Developers official docs + PyPI

gotchas: DO NOT use 'google-generativeai' package - it is deprecated since Nov 2025
Client auto-reads GEMINI_API_KEY env var if no api_key param provided
Structured output requires response_json_schema in config dict, not a separate parameter
System instruction goes in types.GenerateContentConfig, not as a separate parameter
Async requires client.aio namespace (client.aio.models.generate_content)
Streaming uses generate_content_stream() not generate_content(stream=True)

integrations_researched.name: Chutes API (OpenAI-compatible LLM inference)

verified_package.name: openai
verified_package.notes: Chutes exposes an OpenAI-compatible API. Use the standard 'openai' Python package with a custom base_url. The 'chutes' PyPI package (pip install chutes) is the DEPLOYMENT SDK, NOT for inference calls.

api_patterns.verified_against: Chutes official docs + liteLLM integration docs

dependencies: openai (Python package for OpenAI-compatible API calls)

gotchas: Do NOT use the 'chutes' PyPI package for LLM inference - that's the deployment SDK requiring Bittensor wallet
Use the 'openai' package with custom base_url instead
Two base URLs exist: 'https://llm.chutes.ai/v1/' and 'https://api.chutes.ai/v1/' - verify which works for your account
Model names use HuggingFace-style identifiers (e.g., 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1'), not short names
API key is passed via the standard OpenAI client api_key parameter
Streaming is supported via stream=True parameter

integrations_researched.name: Suno AI (prompt format specification)

verified_package.name: N/A - Suno is a prompt format spec, not a library
verified_package.notes: Suno is an AI music generation platform. The project generates Suno-compatible prompts/lyrics, not API calls to Suno directly.

style_prompt.description: Sound definition: genre, tempo-feel, instrumentation, energy, vocal intent, mix direction
style_prompt.formula: genre + subgenre + tempo-feel + core instrumentation + vocal intent + mix intent + emotional axis
style_prompt.example: dreamy bass house, dreamstep influence, 126 bpm feel, deep sub bass, crisp drums, wide synth pads, airy topline, modern clean mix

lyrics_prompt.description: Words and structure only. No sound descriptions or production notes.

best_practices: Keep style prompt about SOUND, lyrics prompt about WORDS and STRUCTURE
Fewer, cleaner signals produce stronger output than many tags
Tag placement matters: top = broad, before section = local and more effective
Avoid sound effects cues in lyrics for cleaner songwriting
Use one variable at a time when iterating
Maintain consistent vocal language across generations
[Intro] can be unreliable - 'short instrumental intro' in style prompt may work better

style_elements: Genres: MPB sacra, sertanejo gospel, folk liturgico brasileiro
Instruments: violao (acoustic guitar), piano, organ, choir
Vocal style: warm, congregational, solemn or joyful depending on context
brazilian_catholic_preset_research.example_style_prompt: Brazilian Catholic hymn, MPB influence, warm acoustic guitar, gentle piano, congregational choir, devotional mood, Portuguese lyrics, reverent and uplifting

gotchas: Suno has NO public API for programmatic music generation - output is prompt text for manual/UI use
Meta tags are suggestions, not commands - Suno may interpret them differently
[Intro] tag is less reliable than describing intro in style prompt
V4.5 changed prompting behavior - older guides may have outdated tag info
Descriptor tags (Mood, Energy, etc.) are experimental and less predictable

integrations_researched.name: Karpathy AutoResearch Pattern

verified_package.name: N/A - architectural pattern, not a library
verified_package.notes: AutoResearch is a design pattern for autonomous iterative improvement, not an installable package.

three_file_structure.editable_asset: Single file the agent modifies (train.py in original)
three_file_structure.instruction_file: program.md - defines objectives, constraints, stopping criteria
three_file_structure.fixed_infrastructure: prepare.py - utilities/evaluation not modified by agent

adaptation_for_musicalizer.editable_asset: The generated song prompt/lyrics (style + lyrics text)

steps: 1. Read current context (preset config, previous eval feedback, generation history)
2. Generate new song prompt/lyrics using LLM provider
3. Evaluate the output (score: good/bad, rationale, improvement suggestions)
5. Keep if improved, discard if not
6. Feed evaluation output as context for next generation
7. Repeat until quality threshold or max iterations reached
key_principles: Single scalar metric for unambiguous comparison
Fixed evaluation criteria for fair comparison across iterations
Each generation uses prior eval as context (learning from mistakes)
Simplicity matters: marginal improvements don't justify complexity
Autonomous operation - no human intervention in the loop

data_model_requirements.generation_lineage: Must track parent-child relationships between generations
data_model_requirements.evaluation_persistence: Must store score, rationale, and improvements for each generation
data_model_requirements.iteration_tracking: Must know which iteration/cycle each generation belongs to

gotchas: Original AutoResearch uses a single numeric metric (val_bpb) - music quality evaluation is more subjective
Must define a clear, parseable evaluation schema for the improvement loop to work
The loop should be bounded (max iterations) unlike the original which runs until interrupted
Evaluation must be done by the LLM itself (no external music analysis tool)
Risk of evaluation/generation model agreement bias - same model may not be harsh enough critic

additional_dependencies.name: pydantic
additional_dependencies.purpose: Schema definition for Preset, Evaluation, and Generation models. Required for structured output with Google GenAI.
additional_dependencies.notes: Core dependency for typed data models. Used for Preset schema, Evaluation output schema, and Gemini structured output via model_json_schema().

additional_dependencies.name: openai
additional_dependencies.purpose: Required for Chutes API integration (OpenAI-compatible endpoint)
additional_dependencies.notes: Used with custom base_url for Chutes provider. Also useful as fallback for any other OpenAI-compatible providers.

unverified_claims.claim: Chutes API supports all models listed on their platform for chat completions inference
unverified_claims.reason: Available models depend on user's Chutes account/subscription. Could not verify full model catalog without authentication.
The URL docs.suno.com referenced in requirements redirects to Suno help center. Official prompt documentation is fragmented across help.suno.com and community resources.

unverified_claims.claim: Suno V4.5 descriptor tags (Mood, Energy, Vocal Style) work reliably
unverified_claims.reason: Community reports suggest these are experimental and inconsistent. Structural tags (Verse, Chorus, etc.) are more reliable.

unverified_claims.claim: LLM self-evaluation produces reliable quality scoring for music prompts
unverified_claims.reason: Using an LLM to both generate and evaluate its own output has inherent bias. No external validation source for music prompt quality exists in this architecture.

recommendations: Use 'google-genai' (NOT 'google-generativeai') as the Gemini SDK - the old package is deprecated
Use the 'openai' package with custom base_url for Chutes API - do NOT use the 'chutes' deployment SDK
Build a provider abstraction (LLMProvider interface) that wraps both google-genai and openai clients
Use Pydantic BaseModel for all schemas: Preset, Evaluation, Generation - enables structured output with Gemini natively
Implement Suno prompt format as a two-field model (style_prompt + lyrics_prompt) matching Suno's actual UI
For the auto-improvement loop, define a clear numeric scoring system (not just good/bad) to enable meaningful comparison between iterations
Bound the auto-improvement loop with max_iterations to prevent runaway API costs
Store full generation lineage (parent generation ID, iteration number, eval results) for the improvement loop
Start with reliable Suno structural tags only ([Verse], [Chorus], [Bridge], [Outro]) - avoid experimental descriptor tags
Consider using a different model for evaluation than generation to reduce self-agreement bias (e.g., generate with Chutes, evaluate with Gemini)

provider_abstraction_design.description: Both providers need a unified interface for the system to be provider-agnostic

chutes_specifics.structured_output: Use system prompt instruction + JSON mode or parse response manually

provider_abstraction_design.abstraction_note: The two SDKs have different interfaces (google-genai vs openai). The provider abstraction must normalize: prompt format (string vs messages array), structured output (native schema vs system prompt), and response parsing.
